title: Printing to PostScript
text: Printing to PostScript To print to PostScript, you need to define a print job model that uses the P_X11 translation table. To print output to PostScript: Define a Print Job Model, setting the properties as follows: Property Value Description Name : Name Enter a meaningful name, such as PS . Device Type : P_X11 For more information, see Device Translation Tables for Printing . Print Queue : Blank, BatchFile or other process Leave this field blank to save the output as a PostScript file with the standard Uniface print file name FormName .P nn . Alternatively, specify a batch file or other print queue that will be used to handle the output. Top Margin : Lines Values for margins to position the output properly. A Top Margin of at least 4 (lines) is recommended. (optional) If you require fonts that are not included in the standard set delivered with Uniface, copy the Adobe Font Metric files ( *.afm ) to the /common/afm directory. If you want to change the fonts and sizes, edit the settings in the [postscript] section of usys.ini . Related Links Define a Print Job Model You must define a print job model before you can print data from Form components. Initiating Printing You initiate the print process from within Uniface using the Proc print command or the ^Print structure editor function. PostScript Support Uniface supports output to PostScript files. PostScript files can be directed to a third-party utility for printing or conversion to PDF. This is useful for batch PDF generation, but also provides enhanced printing capabilities on non-Windows platforms. Font Support for PostScript Output Font rendering for PostScript depends on the font files on your machine. Only Adobe PostScript Type 1 fonts are supported for PostScript printing. [postscript] The [postscript] section of the initialization file defines logical fonts that override those used by default when printing to PostScript on Windows. Was this topic helpful?
